message:
feat(security): argument-aware runtime risk scoring with DCG integration and heredoc AST analysis

Overhauls the extension runtime risk scorer to inspect actual hostcall
arguments instead of relying solely on capability/method names. This
closes a class of evasion where a benign-looking capability (e.g. "exec")
could carry destructive payloads undetected, and reduces false positives
for safe operations like "ls -la" or reading non-sensitive env vars.

Score recalibration:
- Lower base capability scores to make room for argument-level signals:
  exec 0.95→0.48, env 0.85→0.40, http 0.70→0.32, write 0.45→0.28,
  tool 0.50→0.24, session 0.35→0.18, events 0.30→0.11, ui 0.20→0.08,
  read 0.15→0.06, default 0.25→0.12
- Lower method bonuses: exec 0.20→0.10, http 0.12→0.08, tool 0.08→0.04
- Adjust risk formula weights: base 0.65→0.50, recent_mean 0.35→0.30
- Explanation contributors updated to match new weights

Argument signal extraction (RuntimeHostcallArgumentSignals):
- 6 bit-flags: ARG_FLAG_SUSPICIOUS_EXEC, ARG_FLAG_DCG_PATTERN_HIT,
  ARG_FLAG_DCG_HEREDOC_HIT, ARG_FLAG_SENSITIVE_PATH,
  ARG_FLAG_PUBLIC_NETWORK, ARG_FLAG_SECRET_ENV_ACCESS
- risk_delta float clamped to [-0.30, +0.55] added to base score
- Exec commands: classified via existing classify_dangerous_command() plus
  new DCG layer; safe utilities (ls, pwd, echo, cat, etc.) get negative delta
- Paths: sensitive system paths (/etc, /usr, ~/.ssh) add +0.20..0.30;
  traversal (../) adds +0.10; relative paths subtract -0.03
- HTTP: public network targets add +0.14; loopback/private subtract -0.06
- Env vars: secret-pattern keys (TOKEN, PASSWORD, AWS_, OPENAI_, etc.)
  add +0.22; non-secret add +0.06

DCG (Destructive Command Guard) integration:
- runtime_hostcall_dcg_command_score(): string-level scanning for critical
  git commands (reset --hard, clean -fd, push --force, stash clear),
  high-risk git (checkout --, branch -D), and filesystem destruction
  (rm -rf /, dd of=/dev/, mkfs, wipefs, shred). Score capped at 0.55.
- runtime_hostcall_dcg_heredoc_score(): extracts heredoc blocks from exec
  commands, scans each line with DCG command patterns, then performs AST
  analysis for language-specific destructive APIs.

Heredoc AST analysis (ast-grep powered):
- runtime_hostcall_extract_heredoc_blocks(): parses <<DELIMITER heredoc
  syntax, extracting body text and invocation prefix for each block
- Script language detection chain: invocation prefix (python3 <<EOF) →
  shebang (#!/usr/bin/env node) → content heuristics (import shutil)
- Language support: Bash, Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Ruby
- AST patterns per language with severity tiers:
  * Critical: rm -rf, git reset --hard, shutil.rmtree, FileUtils.rm_rf
  * High: os.remove, fs.rmSync, Deno.remove, File.delete, git clean
  * Medium: os.system, subprocess.run, child_process.execSync, system()
- Patterns compiled once via OnceLock and cached for lifetime of process

Harden mode refinement:
- New runtime_risk_harden_should_block_dangerous(): replaces blanket
  "block all dangerous capabilities in harden mode" with trigger-aware
  blocking. Only blocks when risk_score >= 0.82 OR specific high-confidence
  triggers are present (suspicious_exec_detail, dcg_rule_hit,
  dcg_heredoc_hit, sensitive_path_target, public_network_target,
  secret_env_access). This allows benign exec calls (ls, echo) to pass
  through harden mode while still blocking genuinely dangerous ones.
- Escalation bonus in harden+dangerous: now conditional on argument
  signal strength (0.10 if delta >= 0.18, else 0.02)

Trigger improvements:
- 6 new trigger codes from argument signals added to decision triggers
- sensitive_target_mismatch updated to use prefixed resource_target_class
  format (filesystem.*, subprocess.*, network.*, credential.*)

Metadata changes:
- RuntimeRiskCallMetadata gains params: &Value field for argument inspection
- All call sites (dispatch_host_call_shared, evaluate_extension_risk) and
  test fixtures updated to pass params

Tests:
- runtime_risk_dcg_layer_flags_git_reset_hard: DCG detects git reset --hard
- runtime_risk_dcg_heredoc_detects_hidden_destructive_payload: heredoc rm -rf
- runtime_risk_dcg_heredoc_ast_detects_python_delete_api: AST-level shutil
- runtime_risk_argument_signals_reduce_benign_exec_baseline: ls -la reduces
- Golden score tests updated to match recalibrated base scores

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
